title: Herb Roasted Chicken
description: Juicy, herb-butter roasted whole chicken with crispy golden skin. A true Sunday dinner showstopper.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Pat the chicken completely dry with paper towels — this is key for crispy skin.
Mix softened butter with minced garlic, rosemary, thyme, and lemon zest. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Carefully loosen the skin over the breast meat and rub herb butter directly under the skin. Rub remaining butter all over the outside.
Season the cavity with salt and pepper, then stuff with the lemon halves and halved garlic head.
Tie the legs together with kitchen twine. Place the chicken breast-side up in a roasting pan.
Roast for 1 hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 20 minutes, until the juices run clear and a thermometer reads 165°F in the thickest part of the thigh.
Let the chicken rest for at least 10 minutes before carving. Serve with the pan juices.

notes: For the crispiest skin, air-dry the salted chicken uncovered in the fridge overnight before roasting.
